description: executeOnce: after fan-out processing, post one Slack summary for all items — not one message per item.

conversation.text: Every weekday at 9am, fetch this week's open issues from GET https://api.example.com/repos/acme/widgets/issues and post one Slack summary to #eng-bugs listing all of them — not a separate message per issue.

outcomeExpectations: The Slack notification step runs once per workflow execution (executeOnce or equivalent), producing a single digest message that covers multiple issues.
The agent did NOT wire Slack to fire once per issue without collapsing — that would flood the channel with separate messages.

executionScenarios.name: three-issues-one-slack-message
executionScenarios.description: Three issues fetched; exactly one Slack post
executionScenarios.dataSetup: GET https://api.example.com/repos/acme/widgets/issues returns { "issues": [{ "title": "Login 500" }, { "title": "Timezone off by one" }, { "title": "CSV export truncates" }] }. Slack postMessage returns { "ok": true, "ts": "1700000000.0002" }.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. Exactly one Slack message is posted to #eng-bugs and it references all three issue titles (Login 500, Timezone off by one, CSV export truncates). A workflow that posts three separate Slack messages FAILS.
